Notice Of Intention To Move And Mo. To Set Aside And Vacate Order And Enter Another And Different Order
Off Calendar Jul-24-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tommy Angell and Martha Keller for William Shaw. Maria Lawless for Gary Bradley (Conservator). Lawrence Siracusa for Gene Marchi (Trustee). Bruce Feder for Cahterine Minassian.

The Court stated that the Order Appointing Successor Trustee filed on May 15, 2008 was also the Order accepting the resignation of Mr. Shaw. The Court did not allow Mr. Feder and Ms. Lawless to argue as they did not file any oppositions. The Court pointed out that Mr. Siracusa's response was untimely. Ms. Keller stated that she did not want to impede any opposition, but that she did not have proper time to make a response. The Court permitted argument by both parties. After hearing oral argument by Ms. Keller and Mr. Siracusa, the Court denied the Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order. Mr. Siracusa will prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case #290410 (The Catherine Minassian Trust).

Appearances: Tommy Angell and Martha Keller for and with William Shaw (Trustee of The Edward and Louise Minassian Trust). Bruce Feder for Catherine Minassian (Beneficiary). Larry Siracusa for the Trustee. Elvira Orley for Connie Cruz (Prior Trustee of The Catherine Minassian Trust).

Ms. Angell stated that the pleadings stated all the facts and that she had not heard from other Counsel. Mr. Siracusa stated that there were no written objections as there were concerns with additional costs. In addition, he stated that there were concerns with the accounting and suggested the Court allow but not approve the accounting. Mr. Feder agreed with Mr. Siracusa and stated that the concerns were the same as before, that it was not cost efficient to continue to request Mr. Shaw to do the accounting, that the fees were excessive and that the Court should determine the amount of fees. Ms. Angell provided information regarding Mr. Minassian's ability to not maintain good bookeeping records and Ms. Keller explained the relationship of Mr Shaw with the Minassian family. The Court will allow, but not approve the accounting. The fee request was taken under submission. The parties stipulated to having the July 10, 2008 motion continued to July 24, 2008. Mr. Feder will file the petition to remove Mr. Shaw as Trustee from Trust-C which will be heard at the same as the July 10th motion. Mr. Feder will prepare the stipulation and order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR#5229. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris and M. Anderson.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Brunner for Petitioner: William Shaw; Larry Siracusa for and with Successor Trustee: Gene Marchi; Bruce Feder for Beneficiary: Catherine Minassian.

All Counsel presented oral arguments. The Court stated to Mr. Brunner that the supplement on the first accounting was needed. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-21-2008 Continued To May-12-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Larry Siracusa for Gene Marchi (Temp. Successor Trustee); Bruce Feder for Catherine Minassian (Beneficiary); Maria Lawless for Garry Bradley and Randolph Bradley (Conservators); Stephen Brunner and Tommy Angell for William Shaw (Petitioner).

Mr. Siracusa stated that his client signed the consent to serve, but that the Petition for removal was not filed. Mr. Brunner stated that Mr. Shaw's job was not done until the accounting was approved and that the 1st, 2nd and 3rd accounts were not finished.
Mr. Feder stated that it was not unusual for a successor trustee to be appointed, that Mr. Shaw was ready to resign and that it made sense for his client to have Gene Marchi appointed as Successor Trustee on all the cases. The Court directed Mr. Brunner to file a declaration addressing the Examiner's notes. The Court appointed Mr. Marchi as the Successor Trustee. A bond in the amount of $200,000.00 is required. The amended first account is to be filed by May 5, 2008. The matter was continued to May 12, 2008. Mr. Siracusa will prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Guitron.
